observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct: (a) all concerned officers to stop the practice of paying procured materials/supplies through cash advance or reimbursement when direct payment is possible; (b) the concerned Municipal officials and personnel to (i) submit the lacking documents to  the Audit Team and (ii) observe compliance with the provisions of GPPB  Circular in the emergency procurement of goods.
We also recommended that in the distribution of relief goods, Management indicate all the necessary information in the distribution sheet, particularly  the specific goods and quantity of items distributed before causing the signing in the acknowledgement section thereof, to facilitate accounting and establishment of accountability on the goods procured.

We recommended and management agreed that the: a) Accounting Office prepare the BRS of the Municipality; and b) Accounting and Treasury Offices prioritize the reconciliation of their records.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the: a) Accounting Office to maintain separate SLs for all cash accounts of the Municipality and reconcile the same accordingly with the cash control account at the end of each month; and b) Municipal Treasurer to update and balance the Cashbooks daily pursuant to Section 63 of the LTOM.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the procured supplies as inventories in accordance with the procedures prescribed in the Manual; and (ii) exert efforts in the preparation and maintenance of the required SLCs; and (b) MT to prepare the required SCs and regularly coordinate with the Accounting Office on the reconciliation of their records.
We also advised that the LCE (a) constitute an Inventory Committee that will conduct semestral physical count and prepare and submit RPCI thereafter to the Audit Team; and (b) Human Resource Management Officer to find ways on how to address the lack personnel at the Municipal Accounting Office and the MTO.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Designated GSO to consider adopting the one – time cleansing of PPE account balances described under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (b) Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in securing the necessary information on which to base the computation of prior year’s depreciation and henceforth recognize in the books the depreciation cost; and (c) Municipal Assessor and Designated GSO to facilitate the titling of the eight parcels of land in the Municipality name.
We also advised and the LCE concurred to the creation of the Municipal Inventory Committee that will take care of the annual physical count of PPEs and the preparation and submission of the corresponding RPCPPE in the manner prescribed by regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and other concerned offices (i) to facilitate the implementation of pending projects; and (ii) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent development fund in order to comply with the requirement for optimal use and to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; (b) BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents and explanations for the other issues noted; and (c) Municipal Accountant to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment; and (d) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat/concerned officials to submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from execution thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) secure the legislative authority in the use of Special Trust Fund and henceforth refrain from using government funds without an appropriation ordinance; and (b) consider, when funds allow, creating the positions prescribed for the MDRRMO.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) continue to comply with the utilization of GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations; (b) expedite the establishment a centralized GAD Database; (c) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan on the required revision of GAD Code, if any, and take appropriate action thereon.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing tax laws and regulations particularly on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof including government share within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction: The nearest authorized government depository bank is approximately 4 hours away from the Municipality of Dinalungan. Daily deposit will cause excessive costs to the LGU. Moreover, risk of loss or theft along the transport is very high so the management decided to deposit and withdraw from bank irregularly.
observations.reason: Absence of Authorized Government  Depository Bank near the Municipality and Risk of Daily Deposit

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to: (a) find ways how to comply with the prescribed physical validation and reporting procedures for PPE with consideration of health requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense of PPEs procured in the current year and exert efforts in securing the necessary information on which to base the computation of prior year’s depreciation; (c) instruct the Municipal Assessor and Designated General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of the eight parcels of land in the Municipality name; (d) require the designated General Service Officer and Municipal Accountant to (i) provide proof of ownership on the two vehicles as identified in audits; and (ii) see to it that all its motor vehicles are registered annually with the LTO and placed under GSIS insurance coverage; and (e) ascertain/update accountability on said assets.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) stop granting cash advances to employees who were not duly designated and bonded; and (ii) draw payment directly in the name of the suppliers and withhold the appropriate taxes due to the government; (b) BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of GPPB Resolution 03-2020 particularly on the submission of legal, technical and financial documents of the supplier; (c) MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to complete the submission of Relief Distribution List and ensure that all similar distributions are adequately and completely documented to promote transparency and facilitate establishment of accountability on the said activity.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) GSO to strictly adhere to the duties and responsibilities outlined under Section 490(b) of the Local Government Code RA 7160; (b) MDRRMO to furnish the Accounting Office and Committee on Physical Inventory of PPE, a copy of Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and Acknowledgement Receipts for In-kind Donations for DRRM as bases of recording in the agency’s books and reports; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) record In-kind Donations received by the municipality; (ii) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements information pertaining to in-kind donations particularly the quantity received, issued and balance as well as the receipt of donated PPE (if any) and its disposal/transfer.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) coordinate with the Sanggunian for the facilitation of the approval of future appropriation ordinances; and (b) ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) fully capacitate the LDRRM Office and comply with Section 12(b) of the Republic Act No. 10121 and all its legal requirements; and (b) expedite head hunting for the vacant position at the LDRRMO for a more efficient and facilitated delivery of disaster responses. We also suggest that the Management engage the DRRM staff detailed at the Mayor’s Office to perform his mandated duties at the DRRM Office.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the GAD Focal Point System to (a) expedite the establishment of GAD Database; (b) initiate proposal for the enactment of the GAD code; and (c) conduct GAD planning and budgeting based on GAD data base.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed, to comply with the BIR regulations on withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with law and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of Philhealth premium contributions of employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period and recommended that they sustain their very satisfactory observance of related regulations.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

observations.managementAction: Material decrease in cash advances of current employees can be noticed from the financial statements of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) exert efforts in securing the necessary information on which to base the computation of depreciation; and 

(b) henceforth endeavor to present reliable carrying value of PPE accounts through proper and timely recognition of depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation of Municipal Vehicles has been recognized in the CY 2020 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to 

(a) instruct the Local Development Council and the offices concerned to 
(i) determine and accordingly address the causes of the non/delayed implementation of the projects; 

(ii) recommend for the immediate implementation of the same, as warranted; and 

(b) ensure that government funds are timely and efficiently utilized to promote a facilitated delivery of public service.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Municipal Accountant to impose the mandatory Net Take Home Pay to all Municipal employees by limiting the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA.

The identified person’s liable already paid the said disallowance.

observations.reason: Request for write-off of said cash advances is not yet possible due to non-availability of documentary requirements for the write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer 

(a) join efforts in determining the source of funds and cause the return of the idle/unused fund to the source agency; and 

(b) if warranted, request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts in accordance with prescribed procedures.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

 (a) direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to judiciously plan and implement disaster risk management activities to attain the desired goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities; and 

(b)  instruct the MDRRMO to (i) ensure optimal utilization of LDRRMF especially Mitigation Fund to strengthen the local communities’ resilience to future disasters/calamities; and (ii) submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, thru the LDRRMC and LDC, prepared and certified by the Municipal Accountant and in the required format, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive and complete physical count of PPE and (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; 

(b) direct the OIC-General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant to complete/update the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and perform regular reconciliation thereof; and
 
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor and the acting General Services Officer to register in the name of Municipal Government all the lands which are not yet covered by TCT.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 

instruct the BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the implementation of projects and take note of any gaps/issues for future references or subsequent administrative action; an

instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant  to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 for projects involving procurement and submit the lacking documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason:
We have still noted projects under BUB Program which are not yet completed as of December 31, 2018.

Non-adherence with the provisions of RA 9184. 

Lacking documents not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: 1.10	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately issue the final demand letters to the persons accountable for the outstanding cash advances; (b) cause the withholding of their salaries in case of unjustified failure to do so; (c) file appropriate administrative or criminal charges, as warranted; and (d) strengthen its internal control system on the grant and liquidation of cash advances to avoid the recurrence of similar lapses.

2.8	We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert efforts in securing the necessary information on which to base the computation of depreciation; and (b) henceforth endeavor to present reliable carrying value of PPE accounts through proper and timely recognition of depreciation.

3.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Local Development Council and the offices concerned to (i) determine and accordingly address the causes of the non/delayed implementation of the projects; (ii) recommend for the immediate implementation of the same, as warranted; and (b) ensure that government funds are timely and efficiently utilized to promote a facilitated delivery of public service.

6.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Municipal Accountant to impose the mandatory Net Take Home Pay to all Municipal employees by limiting the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA.

7.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and all collectors to prepare the said reports and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA.

8.4	We commended the efforts of the management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with the BIR regulations on withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

9.5	We commended the efforts of the management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended on which the management agreed to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

10.4	We commended the efforts of the management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with with existing rules and regulations on withholding of Philhealth premium contributions of employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period and recommended that they sustain their very satisfactory observance of related regulations.

11.5	We commended the efforts of the management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the management agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

13.4	We reiterated or previous year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to locate documents pertaining to the above disallowances and require the persons liable to settle the audit disallowances as required under Section 10.4 of the RRSA.

We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) exert efforts in securing the necessary documents and reports so that appropriate depreciation can be provided for the identified depreciable assets account and adjustments be made in the government equity account for Prior Years’ depreciation expenses not previously recorded; and 


(b) revisit PPSAS 17 which prescribes the accounting procedures for property, plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer (GSO) already prepared all the necessary tags and stickers for the conduct of physical inventory. The Municipal Accounting Office requested the LCE for an Executive Order creating an Inventory Committee to define the duties and responsibilities of each personnel participating in the conduct of the said physical inventory. Physical Inventory will be conducted twice in 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:

Effect the the adjustment of Loans Payable-Domestic (Current and Non-current) account and Government Equity account pertaining to unrecorded prior year interest expense to reflect the correct amount presented in the financial statements

Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest payments  for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring of loan releases, principal and interest payments.

For future-related transactions, adhere strictly with the provision of above mentioned PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) require the Municipal Engineer and other officers concerned to (i) prioritize fully funded projects and ensure their efficient implementation; and (ii) maximize the utilization of the fund to attain the objective of providing an efficient and effective basic services and facilities to the people; 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account for the 20% Development Fund through the use of complete subsidiary ledger; and 

(c) request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account solely for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to 

(a) facilitate the formulation of a Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) after appropriate coordination with CENRO in accordance with the guidelines set in DAO No. 9 series of 2006; 

(b) submit it to the EMB Regional Office for approval; and 

(c) implement the same as soon as the Authority to Close is received.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe the due dates of submission of the financial reports and documents to the Audit Team and strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to facilitate verification and post-audit activities; and 

(b) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously failed to abide with the timely submission of financial reports and documents.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive properly observe the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 in the creation of GFPS to ensure that gender issues of the Municipality will be  properly addressed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Engineer to submit the  Program of Works, Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and certificate of completion of the implemented projects cited above; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers for construction materials were supported by complete documentation to substantiate payments thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require the members of the Bids and Awards (BAC), the BAC Secretariat to observe strictly the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in every procurement process;

 b)  direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and  

c)   instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason:
Deficiencies were still noted in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects for CY 2018.

Lacking documents not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor


We have still noted transactions with lacking documentation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections daily and intact with the authorized government depository in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS and Section 69(1) of P.D. 1445 to safeguard government money from risk of loss and/or misuse.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive 


(a) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive and complete physical count of PPE and (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; 

(b) direct the OIC-General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant to complete/update the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and perform regular reconciliation thereof; and 

(c) direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provision of PPSAS No. 17 on the provision of depreciation on PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor and the acting General Services Officer to register in the name of Municipal Government all the lands which are not yet covered by TCT.
observations.reason: Non-conduct of physical inventor, hence no Report of Physical Count of PPE

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unsettled cash advances; (ii) deny the granting of cash advances to non-bonded officials and employees; (iii) provide a detailed list of expenses with estimated amounted for cash advances granted for miscellaneous expenses; 

(b) advise the officials concerned to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period and cause the withholding of their salaries in case of unjustified failure to do so; and 

(c) send final demand letters to persons who have retired or already out of the service but still have unliquidated cash advances and undertake appropriate legal actions for its recovery, as warranted.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to the person with outstanding CA but no avail

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the (i) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to indicate in the 20% Development Plan
timelines for the implementation of development projects/programs/activities; and (ii) heads of implementing offices to expedite the implementation and completion of development projects that were not yet started/completed as at December 31, 2017; 

(b) create a project monitoring committee that will (i) closely monitor the implementation of the development projects; and (ii) report to the Local Chief Executive any deviations noted; and 

(c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017

Likewise, we advised that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer not make further disbursements of funds from any of the items of appropriation declared inoperative, disallowed or reduced by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.managementAction: No projects disallowed by SP for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive – 

require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to reconcile the Cash in Bank balances shown in the ledgers and treasury records;

direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant or designated competent Accounting Office personnel to regularly prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements on all depository accounts of the Municipality as required in Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification; and

advise the OIC-Municipal Accountant to recover bank records in prior years or seek assistance of the banks to retrieve necessary records.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2, Part II of this report

instruct the BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the implementation of projects and take note of any gaps/issues for future references or subsequent administrative action; an

instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant  to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 for projects involving procurement and submit the lacking documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We have still noted projects under BUB Program which are not yet completed as of December 31, 2018.

Non-adherence with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs), payrolls, official receipts and report on time as provided in Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed that the Local
Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert efforts in securing
the necessary documents and reports so that appropriate depreciation can be
provided for the identified depreciable assets account and adjustments be made in
the government equity account for Prior Years’ depreciation expenses not
previously recorded; and (b) revisit PPSAS 17 which prescribes the accounting
procedures for property, plant and equipment.

2.11 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct
reconciliation of their respective Cash-in-Bank records at least on a quarterly basis;
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) secure print-out or snap shot copies of the Banks
Statements as an alternative basis in preparing BRS; (ii) immediately prepare bank
reconciliation statements for the early detection of errors that may require
adjustment either in the bank records or in the books of the Municipality; and (iii)
observe the prescribed period in the submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements
to the Office of the Auditor.

3.12 We reiterated our previous year's recommendation and management agreed
that the Municipal Accountant (a) cause the withholding of salaries of those
employees who unjustifiably failed to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances
despite demand; (b) refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable
officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances; (c) file appropriate legal
action, if warranted, against those who have failed to liquidate the overdue cash
advances after exhausting appropriate means, including those who are no longer in
the government service; and (d) request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances
of deceased person

4.10 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Effect the the adjustment of Loans Payable-Domestic (Current and Non-
current) account and Government Equity account pertaining to unrecorded
prior year interest expense to reflect the correct amount presented in the
financial statements
b) Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest payments
for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring of loan releases, principal and
interest payments.
c) For future-related transactions, adhere strictly with the provision of above
mentioned PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

5.5 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) join efforts in
determining the source of funds and cause the return of the idle/unused fund to the
source agency; and (b) if warranted, request for authority to write-off the dormant
accounts in accordance with prescribed procedures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to 

(a) determine the correct amount of RPT and SET collectibles from prior years; 

(b) Municipal Treasurer to henceforth furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayer and the amount due and collectible for the year; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the RPT and SET Receivables in the books based on the said certified list and the list of delinquencies; and (ii) maintain subsidiary records for RPT and SET receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the provision of adequate budget for the bonding of Special Disbursing Officer in the amount that is commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for the current year amounting to ₱4,107,112.10 to a Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the Trust Fund Books to be used solely for disaster risk and management programs and activities within the next five years; (ii) determine the balances of unexpended amount of previous years to be transferred to the Trust Fund book; (iii) disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and (iv) prepare and certify the correctness the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; and (v) disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and 

(b) LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
Transferred on June 29, 2018 amounting to Php9,082,112.12 representing PY unutilized balances.

Separate special trust fund account was already established

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund of the Municipality and implement its identified PPAs incorporated in the LSB Plan for the early enjoyment of benefits of the students.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Engineer to submit the  Program of Works, Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and certificate of completion of the implemented projects cited above; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers for construction materials were supported by complete documentation to substantiate payments thereof.

We recommended that the management render an explanation or valid justification on why it resorted to alternative method of procurement particularly “Shopping” without proper observance of the guidelines set forth in Rule XVI Section 48 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the grant of additional bonuses and other personnel benefits without any legal basis without prejudice to the settlement of any administrative case that resulted from these irregular payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) LCPC to (i) submit the approved CY 2017 LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan and other required reports/documents as listed in DILG Memorandum No 2012-120 and (ii) formulate programs that would contribute to the attainment of objectives of LCPC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require the members of the Bids and Awards (BAC), the BAC Secretariat to observe strictly the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in every procurement process;

 b)  direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and  

c)   instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

Deficiencies were still noted in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects for CY 2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

comply with the essential elements of GAD Planning and Budgeting to be able to identify, prioritize and address gender issues and gaps.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the budget year; and 

require the concerned officers to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Still reported a low utilization rate of 50.31% for CY 2017. Reiterated in Finding No. 5 , Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office and the DRRM Officer prepare and submit regularly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and disclose the details of the unexpended balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements as of the end of the accounting period.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the required reports for CY 2017. Reiterated in Finding No. 6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is fully obligated/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7. Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
1.15	We reiterated our previous recommendations and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive and complete physical count of PPE and (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) direct the OIC-General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant to complete/update the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and perform regular reconciliation thereof; and (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provision of PPSAS No. 17 on the provision of depreciation on PPE accounts.

1.16	We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Assessor and the acting General Services Officer to register in the name of Municipal Government all the lands which are not yet covered by TCT.

2.8	 We reiterated our previous recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unsettled cash advances; (ii) deny the granting of cash advances to non-bonded officials and employees; (iii) provide a detailed list of expenses with estimated amounted for cash advances granted for miscellaneous expenses; (b) advise the officials concerned to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period and cause the withholding of their salaries in case of unjustified failure to do so; and (c) send final demand letters to persons who have retired or already out of the service but still have unliquidated cash advances and undertake appropriate legal actions for its recovery, as warranted.

3.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) determine the correct amount of RPT and SET collectibles from prior years; (b) Municipal Treasurer to henceforth furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayer and the amount due and collectible for the year; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the RPT and SET Receivables in the books based on the said certified list and the list of delinquencies; and (ii) maintain subsidiary records for RPT and SET receivables.

4.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to cause the provision of adequate budget for the bonding of Special Disbursing Officer in the amount that is commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

5.12	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the (i) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to indicate in the 20% Development Plan timelines for the implementation of development projects/programs/activities; and (ii) heads of implementing offices to expedite the implementation and completion of development projects that were not yet started/completed as at December 31, 2017; (b) create a project monitoring committee that will (i) closely monitor the implementation of the development projects; and (ii) report to the Local Chief Executive any deviations noted; and (c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017
5.13	Likewise, we advised that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer not make further disbursements of funds from any of the items of appropriation declared inoperative, disallowed or reduced by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

6.9	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for the current year amounting to ₱4,107,112.10 to a Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the Trust Fund Books to be used solely for disaster risk and management programs and activities within the next five years; (ii) determine the balances of unexpended amount of previous years to be transferred to the Trust Fund book; (iii) disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and (iv) prepare and certify the correctness the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; and (v) disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and (b) LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

6.10	We also recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to open a separate special trust fund account solely for the unexpended balances of LDRRMF.

7.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund of the Municipality and implement its identified PPAs incorporated in the LSB Plan for the early enjoyment of benefits of the students.

8.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Engineer to submit the  Program of Works, Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and certificate of completion of the implemented projects cited above; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers for construction materials were supported by complete documentation to substantiate payments thereof.

9.5	We recommended that the management render an explanation or valid justification on why it resorted to alternative method of procurement particularly “Shopping” without proper observance of the guidelines set forth in Rule XVI Section 48 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

10.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to stop the grant of additional bonuses and other personnel benefits without any legal basis without prejudice to the settlement of any administrative case that resulted from these irregular payments.

11.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) LCPC to (i) submit the approved CY 2017 LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan and other required reports/documents as listed in DILG Memorandum No 2012-120 and (ii) formulate programs that would contribute to the attainment of objectives of LCPC.

12.5   We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue being compliant with the revenue regulations on the withholding of taxes from the salaries and wages of personnel and payments to suppliers/contractors for the purchase of goods and infrastructure projects and the remittance thereof to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously being compliant with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously being compliant with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF contributions and repayments of loan amortization from salary of personnel and on remittance thereof to the HDMF within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive – 

require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to reconcile the Cash in Bank balances shown in the ledgers and treasury records;

direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant or designated competent Accounting Office personnel to regularly prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements on all depository accounts of the Municipality as required in Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification; and

advise the OIC-Municipal Accountant to recover bank records in prior years or seek assistance of the banks to retrieve necessary records.
observations.reason: Alternative action has already been taken such as sending of request for snap shots of bank statements and application for the viewing access at LBP website called WeAccess for the timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation. For CY 2017, Bank Reconciliation Statements of all accounts were not prepared/submitted to the office of the auditor due to delay in the releasing of bank statements.

Reiterated in Finding No.2, Part II of this report.



Incomplete documentation of advances by the SDO


Non-preparation of Subsidiary Ledger by the Accounting Personnel for Advances to Disbursing Officer.


Non-conduct of  regular reconciliation by the Accounting Staff and Disbursing Officer

Due to insufficiency of necessary documents for the disposition/closure of dormant accounts.


Reiterated in Finding No.2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of PPE and submit a report thereon;

require the acting Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of PPE account; and (ii) to comply with the provision of PPSAS No. 17 on the provision of depreciation on PPE accounts; and

direct the acting GSO to likewise maintain property card for property, plant and equipment and reconcile the balance per property cards with the ledger cards of the accounting office.
observations.reason:
Incomplete physical count of PPE for CY 2017 and non-subission of RCPPE.

Non-maintenance of property records by the Municipal Accountant and the acting GSO due to manpower constraints. Likewise, provision of depreciation was not yet effected. Reiterated in Finding No.3, Part II of this report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

require the members of the Bids and Awards (BAC), the BAC Secretariat to observe strictly the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in every procurement process;

 b)  direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and  

c)   instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections daily and intact with the authorized government depository in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS and Section 69(1) of P.D. 1445 to safeguard government money from risk of loss and/or misuse.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

follow-up the approval of the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; and

undertake best practices in adhering to and implementing the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and its IRR.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of paying fixed allowances or fixed reimbursements for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees was already effected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

follow-up the approval of the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; and

undertake best practices in adhering to and implementing the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and its IRR.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure proper allocation of fund and formulation of GAD Plan and Budget on implementation of GAD operations in the Municipality and instruct the focal point to properly monitor the GAD activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs), payrolls, official receipts and report on time as provided in Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: There was still delay in the submission of vouchers for the period Jan. to December 2017 due to manpower constraints.

observations.managementAction: No action was taken
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (i) prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality (ii) trace the cause/s of the discrepancy between the general ledger and bank statements and effect the necessary adjustments/ corrections as maybe necessary to bring the two records into agreement and (iii) communicate with the bank concerned, all identified bank reconciling items, so that necessary adjustments, if warranted, or other appropriate actions could be taken by the bank.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken
observations.reason: Absence of records and no permanent personnel knowledgeable in the preparation of BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management instruct the officials and employees with outstanding cash advance to immediately liquidate or settle the same and direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to withhold salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The LGU management issued demand letters to all accountable officers.
observations.reason: Absence of accounting records

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the (a) MPDC and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions can be introduced at the earliest time possible; and (b) the members of the BAC schedule early all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for their timely implementation and completion.
observations.managementAction: The same observation was noted for CY 2016, hence reiterated in Part II of the report.
observations.reason: Due to transition period as a result of the local election.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure proper allocation of fund and formulation of GAD Plan and Budget on implementation of GAD operations in the Municipality and instruct the focal point to properly monitor the GAD activities.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated funds for GAD activities by attribution to different offices. However, the PAPs included were regularly performed by the different offices.
No significant PPAs was accomplished for SEF was noted for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation of SEF budget because the LSB was not furnished with the certification of available resources necessary in the preparation of the budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive –
require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to reconcile the Cash in Bank balances shown in the ledgers and treasury records;
direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant or designated competent Accounting Office personnel to regularly prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements on all depository accounts of the Municipality as required in Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification; and
advise the OIC-Municipal Accountant to recover bank records in prior years or seek assistance of the banks to retrieve necessary records.
1.6	With the appointment of a new Municipal Accountant, the management assured compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of PPE and submit a report thereon;
require the acting Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of PPE account; and (ii) to comply with the provision of PPSAS No. 17 on the provision of depreciation on PPE accounts; and
direct the acting GSO to likewise maintain property card for property, plant and equipment and reconcile the balance per property cards with the ledger cards of the accounting office.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant explained that his Office has done adequate efforts to retrieve records and documents containing necessary information regarding the valuation of all PPE and in order to provide for depreciation. Due to frequent turnover of personnel in-charged in the property section and the absence of proper turnover of documents and PPE ledger cards, the issue has not been resolved. However, the Municipal Mayor had already designated the newly appointed Supply Officer as acting GSO to work with the Accounting Office and they scheduled the inventory taking in the 2nd quarter of 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the members of the Bids and Awards (BAC), the BAC Secretariat to observe strictly the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in every procurement process; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and  (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and the DRRM Officer prepare and submit regularly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and disclose the details of the unexpended balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements as of the end of the accounting period.
The Accounting Office admitted their lapses and assured the Audit Team that the required report shall be prepared monthly in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is fully obligated/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections daily and intact with the authorized government depository in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS and Section 69(1) of P.D. 1445 to safeguard government money from risk of loss and/or misuse.
9.6	The Municipal Treasurer reasoned out that she could not comply with the regulation on daily deposit of collections due to lack of available service vehicle considering the distance and risks of going to and from their authorized depository bank.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) follow-up the approval of the Municipality’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; and (b) undertake best practices in adhering to and implementing the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare its GAD Plan and allocate the budget pursuant to DBM/NEDA/NCRFW Joint Circular No. 01; (b) and comply with the essential elements of GAD Planning and Budgeting to be able to identify, prioritize and address gender issues and gaps.
Management noted the audit recommendations and promised to consider the same in the future implementation of GAD Programs.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of paying fixed allowances or fixed reimbursements for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees.
The LGU management acknowledged our observation and the Audit Team was assured of compliance with the audit recommendation.
We recommended that management continuously comply with and adhere to the GSIS law on withholding and remitting the amount due to GSIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to comply on BIR regulation on the withholding of taxes due and prompt remittance thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the person responsible to the disallowances to immediately settle the same as required in Section 10.4 of the RRSA.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer implement at earliest possible time infrastructure projects related to disaster prevention and mitigation; and (b) DRRM Officer monitor and evaluate the utilization of Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund based on the PPAs listed in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: PAPs under LDRRMF Plan were implemented. However, the reporting guidelines for DRRM were not complied, hence included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE agreed to appropriate adequate funding and prioritize the implementation of programs identified in the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (MSWMP) and the environmental programs.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued a memo on the strict implementa- tion of environment-tal programs of the LGU.
observations.reason: A sanitary landfill as a disposal facility is not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs), payrolls, official receipts and report on time as provided in Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: There was still delay in the submission of vouchers for the period Jan. to March 2016 and year-end financial reports
observations.reason: Due to manpower constraints
